# nix

All options under `nix`.

| Option | Type | Description |
| ------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `nix.allowedUsers` | | |
| `nix.autoOptimiseStore` | | |
| `nix.binaryCachePublicKeys` | | |

| `nix.buildCores` | | |
| `nix.buildMachines` | `list of (submodule)` | This option lists the machines to be used if distributed builds are enabled (see nix.distributedBuilds ). Nix will perform derivations on those machines via SSH by copying the inputs to the Nix store on the remote machine, starting the build, then copying the output back to the local Nix store. |
| `nix.channel.enable` | `boolean` | Whether the nix-channel command and state files are made available on the machine. |
| `nix.checkConfig` | `boolean` | If enabled (the default), checks for data type mismatches and that Nix can parse the generated nix.conf. |
| `nix.configureBuildUsers` | | |
| `nix.daemonCPUSchedPolicy` | | |
| `nix.daemonIOLowPriority` | `boolean` | Whether the Nix daemon process should considered to be low priority when doing file system I/O. |
| `nix.daemonIONice` | | |
| `nix.daemonIOSchedClass` | | |
| `nix.daemonIOSchedPriority` | | |
| `nix.daemonNiceLevel` | | |
| `nix.daemonProcessType` | `one of “Background”, “Standard”, “Adaptive”, “Interactive”` | Nix daemon process resource limits class. These limits propagate to build processes. Standard is the default process type and will apply light resource limits, throttling its CPU usage and I/O bandwidth. |
| `nix.distributedBuilds` | `boolean` | Whether to distribute builds to the machines listed in nix.buildMachines . |
| `nix.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable Nix. |
| `nix.envVars` | | |
| `nix.extraOptions` | `strings concatenated with “\n”` | Additional text appended to nix.conf . |
| `nix.gc.automatic` | `boolean` | Automatically run the garbage collector at a specific time. |
| `nix.gc.dates` | | |
| `nix.gc.interval` | `(submodule) or unique (non-empty (list of (submodule)))` | The calendar interval at which the garbage collector will run. See the serviceConfig.StartCalendarInterval option of the launchd module for more info. |
| `nix.gc.options` | `string` | Options given to nix-collect-garbage when the garbage collector is run automatically. |
| `nix.gc.persistent` | | |
| `nix.gc.randomizedDelaySec` | | |
| `nix.gc.user` | | |
| `nix.linux-builder.config` | `module` | This option specifies extra NixOS configuration for the builder. You should first use the Linux builder without changing the builder configuration otherwise you may not be able to build the Linux builder. |
| `nix.linux-builder.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable Linux builder. |
| `nix.linux-builder.ephemeral` | `boolean` | Whether to enable wipe the builder’s filesystem on every restart. |
| `nix.linux-builder.mandatoryFeatures` | `list of string` | A list of features mandatory for the Linux builder. The builder will be ignored for derivations that don’t require all features in this list. All mandatory features are automatically included in supportedFeatures . |
| `nix.linux-builder.maxJobs` | `positive integer, meaning >0` | Instead of setting this directly, you should set nix.linux-builder.config.virtualisation.cores to configure the amount of cores the Linux builder should have. |
| `nix.linux-builder.modules` | | |
| `nix.linux-builder.package` | `package` | This option specifies the Linux builder to use. |
| `nix.linux-builder.protocol` | `string` | The protocol used for communicating with the build machine. Use ssh-ng if your remote builder and your local Nix version support that improved protocol. |
| `nix.linux-builder.speedFactor` | `positive integer, meaning >0` | The relative speed of the Linux builder. This is an arbitrary integer that indicates the speed of this builder, relative to other builders. Higher is faster. |
| `nix.linux-builder.supportedFeatures` | `list of string` | A list of features supported by the Linux builder. The builder will be ignored for derivations that require features not in this list. |
| `nix.linux-builder.systems` | `list of string` | This option specifies system types the build machine can execute derivations on. |
| `nix.linux-builder.workingDirectory` | `string` | The working directory of the Linux builder daemon process. |
| `nix.maxJobs` | | |
| `nix.nixPath` | `nix path` | The default Nix expression search path, used by the Nix evaluator to look up paths enclosed in angle brackets (e.g. <nixpkgs> ). |
| `nix.nrBuildUsers` | `signed integer` | Number of nixbld user accounts created to perform secure concurrent builds. If you receive an error message saying that “all build users are currently in use”, you should increase this value. |
| `nix.optimise.automatic` | `boolean` | Automatically run the nix store optimiser at a specific time. |
| `nix.optimise.dates` | | |
| `nix.optimise.interval` | `(submodule) or unique (non-empty (list of (submodule)))` | The calendar interval at which the optimiser will run. See the serviceConfig.StartCalendarInterval option of the launchd module for more info. |
| `nix.optimise.user` | | |
| `nix.package` | `package` | This option specifies the Nix package instance to use throughout the system. |
| `nix.profile` | | |
| `nix.readOnlyStore` | | |
| `nix.registry` | `attribute set of (submodule)` | A system-wide flake registry. |
| `nix.requireSignedBinaryCaches` | | |
| `nix.sandboxPaths` | | |
| `nix.settings` | `open submodule of attribute set of (Nix config atom (null, bool, int, float, str, path or package) or list of (Nix config atom (null, bool, int, float, str, path or package)))` | Configuration for Nix, see https://nixos.org/manual/nix/stable/#sec-conf-file for avalaible options. The value declared here will be translated directly to the key-value pairs Nix expects. |
| `nix.trustedBinaryCaches` | | |
| `nix.trustedUsers` | | |
| `nix.useDaemon` | | |
| `nix.useSandbox` | | |
